summary: Draft, closed-unmerged PR that advanced decompilation of the name-entry/name-list menu area. It replaced several mnname.c placeholders with C implementations, tightened mnname.h and related prototypes, and moved name persistent-data access from raw offsets to gmm_x2FF8_inner fields. The decomp.dev report showed 3 new 100% matches, IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, and mnName_8023A058, plus 9 partial improvements in mnname functions, for +316 matched bytes. There was no human review text in the slice, so intent beyond the diff and bot report is not evidenced.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work; added implementations for name-list helpers, input dispatcher, page/column counting, JObj animation/cleanup helpers, and disallowed-name checks.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures for mnName functions.
Typed InitializePersistentNameData as void InitializePersistentNameData(u8 slot).
Added include of mnname.h, likely to satisfy newly typed cross-file declarations.

what_changed: Implemented mnName_8023749C to select between US and non-US name text tables using lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS, scan entries until the string terminator, and return NULL when the indexed entry is the terminator.
Changed GetNameText and IsNameValid to use GetPersistentNameData(slot)->namedata instead of pointer arithmetic at offset 0x198.
Implemented CreateNameAtIndex to initialize a name slot by setting namedata[0] to mnName_StringTerminator, setting x1A1 to 1, and calling InitializePersistentNameData(slot).
Implemented fn_80238540 as a switch dispatcher over mn_804A04F0.x10, calling mnName_MainInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, or mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput.
Implemented mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount by counting valid persistent names over 0x78 slots and rounding up by page sizes 24 and 6 respectively.
Added local structs MnName88D4Data and MnNameData to model menu object/JObj-related data enough for matching helper functions.
Implemented mnName_802388D4 to retrieve child/next JObj entries for indices below 0x18 and special fields x24, x18, x1C for indices 0x18 through 0x1A.
Implemented mnName_80238A04 to request and apply animations on name menu JObjs using mnName_80238964 and mnName_803ED538 animation-frame table entries.
Implemented mnName_80239EBC and mnName_80239F5C to set JObj translate.z and translate.x, preserve assert behavior, and mark matrices dirty unless flags suppress it.
Implemented mnName_80239FFC and mnName_8023A058 cleanup paths that remove child JObjs and free SisLib resources via HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4; mnName_8023A058 then calls mnName_80239A24.
Implemented IsNameNotAllowed by iterating NotAllowedNamesList until mnNameNew_NullCharacter and using CompareNameStrings to detect banned names.
Updated function declarations across mnname.h, mnnamenew.h, and gmmain_lib.h to remove broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where argument and return types were inferred.
smart_moves: Using the existing gmm_x2FF8_inner structure and namedata/x1A1 fields made persistent name manipulation clearer and less brittle than raw byte offsets.
Typing InitializePersistentNameData(u8 slot) and mnNameNew_MainInput(void) enabled cleaner cross-module calls from the newly implemented mnname code.

The page and column count implementations deliberately recompute validity from the persistent name terminator, matching the existing IsNameValid semantics rather than introducing a separate cached count.
The JObj translate helpers preserved the original assert and flag-check patterns before calling HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub, which is often important for matching baselib-adjacent code.
For table scanning, mnName_8023749C retained explicit terminator handling and language-table selection, avoiding assumptions that every index maps to a valid string.
The banned-name check used the same CompareNameStrings routine as the rest of the name system, avoiding duplicate string comparison semantics.
decomp_lessons: When a persistent save/menu struct is partially known, replacing magic offsets with named fields can unlock multiple small matches and improve nearby header types.
Menu name code uses sentinel characters heavily: mnName_StringTerminator marks empty persistent name slots and mnNameNew_NullCharacter terminates the not-allowed-name list.
For name-list UI counts, evidence in this PR points to 0x78 total name slots, 24 names per page, and 6 names per column/page subdivision.
Input dispatcher functions can often be matched as simple state switches over global menu state; here mn_804A04F0.x10 selected main, new-name, or delete-confirmation input.
HSD_JObj child traversal patterns should preserve NULL checks before walking child/next chains; this helped implement mnName_802388D4.
JObj property setters in Melee often include assert-line artifacts and matrix-dirty flag logic; keep those details rather than simplifying them away if matching is the goal.
Local temporary structs with only known offsets, such as MnName88D4Data and MnNameData, are useful stepping stones, but should be revisited once the enclosing menu data layout is better understood.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for what actually matched: in this slice only IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, and mnName_8023A058 reached 100%; other added functions remained partial.
naming_conventions: Existing exported symbols keep address-based names when behavior is not yet understood, e.g. mnName_8023749C, mnName_802388D4, mnName_80239EBC.
Readable names are used for understood public helpers, e.g. GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameValid, CreateNameAtIndex, IsNameNotAllowed.
Struct field names remain offset-based when semantics are unknown, e.g. x14, x18, x1C, x24, x30, x3C, while known persistent-name fields use namedata and x1A1.
Local variables in the diff often encode matching intent or observed roles, e.g. first_char, terminator, nullChar, firstChar, table, ptr, rem.
Header prototypes follow exact C types once known: bool for predicates, void for procedures, s32 for integer return helpers, u8 for slot/index bytes, and HSD_GObj*/HSD_JObj* for object APIs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit goto labels in mnName_8023749C to reproduce a table-scanning loop shape.
Introduced extra locals such as zero, valid, rem, term, and first_char in count functions, likely to guide register allocation and control-flow shape.
Preserved repeated NULL checks and assert calls in JObj setters, even where they look redundant in source, because they reflect baselib macro/codegen patterns.
Used casts between void*, s32, HSD_JObj*, and local struct types where the decompilation still lacked final data types but needed matching-compatible access.
Implemented modulo-based round-up as count / page_size plus a conditional remainder flag instead of using a helper macro or arithmetic shortcut.
Kept table lookup and animation calls explicit in mnName_80238A04, including f32 animation-frame table mnName_803ED538 and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll sequencing.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed +0.01% matched code and +316 bytes.
New 100% matches: IsNameNotAllowed (+116 bytes), CreateNameAtIndex (+100 bytes), and mnName_8023A058 (+100 bytes).
Partial improvements remained for mnName_80238A04, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_8023749C, mnName_80239EBC, mnName_80239F5C, mnName_802388D4, fn_80238540, and mnName_80239FFC.
No human issue comments, review comments, or review approvals/changes were present in the provided slice; PR was draft and closed without merge.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged or replaced this mnname work, especially implementations for the partial functions reported here.
Search for final definitions of gmm_x2FF8_inner to confirm namedata and x1A1 offsets used by GetPersistentNameData.
Look for review or commit history around mnName_80239EBC and mnName_80239F5C to see how the remaining JObj setter mismatches were resolved.
Search for NotAllowedNamesList and CompareNameStrings to document the exact name-string comparison and banned-name table format.
Check whether local structs MnName88D4Data and MnNameData were later promoted to shared headers or renamed with semantic field names.
